reason: Both correctly land on fix-don't-kill with accurate LTV:CAC (2.2) and payback (~6-7.5mo) math, and both give ordered, actionable fix plans. B is more thorough on the key points: it explicitly calls out blended-vs-paid CAC, and its per-intent landing page fix covers both intent-tier segmentation and landing-page/offer match. B also adds a correct, valuable insight - inferring ~7-8%/mo churn from the LTV/price ratio, showing retention may be the real bottleneck - plus a caution about small monthly sample size. A is slightly better on comparing to compounding-return channels (naming SEO/outbound as alternatives) but misses the blended-CAC point and is less explicit on intent segmentation. Neither covers branded-vs-generic split. B covers more of the rubric's points with added correct insight.
